Tools, Part 1
OK, so now I want to talk about some of the tools that we use in our launches. These
are tools that we use and recommend. In this video I’m going to cover launch pages,
funnel pages, webpages, video pages, and all those types of things. How do we actually
build the launch that everyone is going to see?
A few ground rules here…
First of all, the great thing is that these tools just keep on getting better. A few years
ago there weren't any of these tools, and you had to build all your pages from scratch
and it was a nightmare. That's not something I do and we'd always be trying to find
someone to design our pages that understood this. It was a big pain. A number of
years ago, some of these tools starting coming out and it made our lives a lot better. All
of a sudden our pages started looking a lot better. The great news is that some of these
tools are actually inexpensive, especially considering what you get.
You end up with a professional looking website and launch funnel sales page. The tools
keep on getting better, and compared to a year ago, way better. Compared to two years
ago, huge amounts better, and that’s really fantastic.
Keep in mind, this list is not comprehensive. There are a lot of different tools. New stuff
is coming out all the time. I'm mostly going to focus on tools that I use, my team uses,
or the few that my guru buddies (or my platinum mastermind) uses that I feel
comfortable recommending, and I've heard so many good things about. If you don't
see something on this list it doesn't mean it doesn't work. It just means that
I don't have experience with it and we'll leave it at that.
One important thing is this idea of “horses for courses”. I don't know if this is an
Australian term, but I heard it from my Australian friend so let’s assume it is. The idea is
that in horse racing certain horses are better for certain courses, compared to other
courses.
The same thing applies with a lot of things, like boats. I’m a big fan of boats, and I
spend a lot of time on them. If you want to cross the Atlantic Ocean you're probably
going to have a different type of boat than if you just want to take a summer family
cruise around the lake, or if you want to kayak down a white water river. There's
different boats for different rivers, lakes, and oceans.
There's different horses for different courses and there's different tools that are better
for different situations. Just because you see me using a specific tool doesn't mean
that's the one that you should automatically be using. Remember that I’ve built up my
business now, I can afford to spend more money. I've got a team that runs things. So,
just because I'm using something doesn't make it the perfect fit for you. The other
thing is all these tools have their own tutorials built in so I'm not going to give you
tutorials on these specific tools because that'd be crazy for me to duplicate their efforts

-- 2 of 4 --

when they know how to use their stuff so much better. Their tools is changing all the
time, so there's no way I can keep up tutorials for all these different tools.
These are recommendations. Some of them I'll be stronger on than others. Some of
them will have affiliate links. Many of them won't have affiliate links. In general, I’m just
telling you what I think about these tools.
So for creating squeeze pages and launch funnels - Leadpages (http://
www.sixinseven.com/leadpages). I absolutely love, love Leadpages. This is how we
create pretty much all the pages for our business right now. It's great for squeeze
pages, sales pages, webinar pages, webcast pages, thank you pages, etc. They keep on
adding more pages. They're tested designs that can integrate with WordPress,
Facebook, and so on.
You can build standalone pages that can be loaded onto your site, or you can host a
page with them. It's enormously flexible. A disclaimer here. It's run by Clay Collins, a
longtime friend of mine. He's been out to my house. He came out here for a birthday
party of mine once. He’s hung out with my family, and I’ve been in a mastermind with
Clay for years, so he's a good friend. I wouldn't recommend Leadpages or use it if it
wasn't a great tool - and it is. The thing about Clay is he is a hardcore marketer, direct
marketer, online marketer, whatever you want to call him. He really pays attention to
testing and what works.
They're always testing these pages and watching the data from all their clients. They
literally know what works best. I've used this format for two consecutive launches right
now. I just go to Clay and say "Hey, we want to build out a launch. What's working best
right now?" He'll literally say "This is the design that's working the best." Those are the
designs that were used that they feature on their site, so I am a huge fan of
Leadpages. There's a link you can get to at http://www.sixinseven.com/leadpages.
Again, I love, love, love Leadpages. You should definitely check it out. For the value that
you get it's an incredible incredibly well-priced tool. Huge value. You can just basically
have a professional looking web presence and launch based on just using their stuff,
and it's all fill in the blank. It's super easy to use. I can't recommend Leadpages more
highly. It's an awesome tool.
Now there are other tools that work for this - Kajabi (http://www.sixinseven.com/
kajabi). We've used Kajabi for more than three years. It has a great service. The PLF
portal that you're in right now is built using Kajabi. It's amazing for online video
courses. We love it for big comprehensive courses, it excels at doing a large course like
PLF. It can also do smaller courses really well so we really like it.
Right now we're using Leadpages for our launch pages, for our launch funnels, and our
squeeze pages. Then we're using Kajabi to deliver the product. Now you can also use
Kajabi to build your launch funnels and your squeeze pages and we did that for a
number of years. Right now we've gone to lead pages. It's a little bit more flexible. If
you want to do everything in one place Kajabi will do it for you. It's plug and play.

-- 3 of 4 --

Pretty darn simple to use. Maybe not as customizable as Leadpages, and you can get
there at http://www.sixinseven.com/kajabi. We’ve been sending people to Kajabi for
years and feel really great about that.
There's also OptimizePress (http://www.sixinseven.com/optimizepress). I've never used
this one myself. My team might have used it for some pages but I know a lot people
really liked it. It's good for squeeze pages and I also think there's launch funnel pages
there. You can get there at http://www.sixinseven.com/optimizepress.
Then WordPress is just great free software. I mean years ago it was just for building
blogs. Then it became a comprehensive CMS (Content Management System). It's a
great place to start. That's what my blog at JeffWalker.com is built on. WordPress is sort
of the bare bones, and then there's all kinds of software that you can add on to it that
will give you different themes.
There's all kinds of plug ins that will give you different functionality, so it's super
customizable, it's free, and it's fantastic. The other cool thing is when you build your
site on WordPress than the pages you build and Leadpages can get added straight into
that WordPress site. The same thing with OptimizePress. They both build pages that
integrate right into WordPress. You can actually build your overall site on WordPress
and then use Leadpages or OptimizePress to integrate with it. Check that out at https://
wordpress.org/.
Overall it's just so much better to be in this business now than it was a few years ago,
because it's so much easier to build these pages. Instead of building them from scratch,
you can use Leadpages and Kajabi and have a great looking launch funnel and squeeze
page all built very quickly.
You can log in there, change your headline, and see what it looks like. It's beautiful. If
you want to build a course similar to PLF, the way I laid this out, it's just super easy in
Kajabi. Before Kajabi, it would take us an entire day to build out a module and all the
links. It would take a day to build it and another half day to test it. Now we can do that
within probably an hour's time frame in Kajabi. You have the tools now, and they keep
on getting better.
